statement.text: Short term oil prices, which will drop rapidly when the destruction of the Iran nuclear threat is over, is a very small price to pay for U.S.A., and World, Safety and Peace. ONLY FOOLS WOULD THINK DIFFERENTLY!

evidence.claim: Oil prices surged 36% in one week following the Iran war, with Brent crude exceeding $100/barrel
U.S. employers cut more jobs than they created in February 2026
Energy Secretary Chris Wright stated price impacts would last 'weeks, certainly not months' — contradicted by analysts projecting Brent could reach $120 if disruption persists

strike: You said oil prices are 'a very small price to pay.' Brent crude surged 36% in one week to over $100/barrel. U.S. jobs declined in February. Your own Energy Secretary said this would last 'weeks not months' — analysts say it could hit $120. For the families paying $6/gallon this week, which part is 'very small'?
